name: test-scaffolding
description: Generate test file scaffolds from source analysis with language-appropriate templates.
capabilities: Analyzes source files to extract public functions, classes, and methods
Detects test framework from project manifest files
Generates test scaffolds with TODO stubs for test-engineer agents
Supports 6 languages: Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, Rust, Dart
Applies language-specific naming conventions and import patterns
Returns JSON manifest with generated files and unit counts
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: This skill is a pure prompt/skill definition containing only markdown documentation and test templates. The scanner flagged markdown syntax (backticks), SHA256 content hashes, and relative import paths as security issues. All findings are false positives - backticks are markdown code formatting, hashes are file integrity checks, and '../' paths are legitimate relative import syntax. No executable code, network calls, or file system operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Writing test files from scratch slows down test-driven development. This skill analyzes your source code and generates test scaffolds with TODO stubs in your preferred framework, so you can focus on implementing tests instead of setting them up.
userTitle: Generate test scaffolds for source files
limitations: Does not execute tests or run test frameworks
Requires an AI agent to read source files and apply templates
Scaffolds are starting points that need human or agent implementation
Framework detection requires package manifest files

useCases.title: Accelerate TDD workflows
useCases.description: Quickly generate test scaffolds so you can focus on implementing test logic instead of setup.

useCases.title: Enable automated testing
useCases.description: Agents use this skill to auto-populate tests for owned files during lane execution.

useCases.title: Standardize test patterns
useCases.description: Ensure consistent test file structure across your codebase with framework-specific templates.

promptTemplates.title: Basic scaffold request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use test-scaffolding skill to generate test scaffolds for src/auth/login.py and src/auth/user.ts. Use pytest for Python. Output to tests/auth/.

promptTemplates.title: Framework specification
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate test scaffolds for src/utils/calculator.ts using Vitest framework. Include fixture stubs. Output to tests/unit/utils/.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-file batch
promptTemplates.prompt: Use test-scaffolding to analyze src/api/ directory. Generate test scaffolds for all TypeScript files with Jest framework. Skip files with existing tests.

promptTemplates.title: Integration with plan
promptTemplates.prompt: From the plan output, identify files marked as Tests Owned. Generate test scaffolds for those files using the detected framework. Return the scaffold manifest.

bestPractices: Use TODO stubs so AI agents can detect and fill in test implementations
Apply framework detection automatically from project manifests
Mirror source directory structure in test directories for clarity
Skip files with existing tests to avoid duplicate work
antiPatterns: Manually creating test files from scratch instead of using scaffolds
Overwriting existing test files without checking for changes
Using skip markers instead of TODO comments for unimplemented tests
Ignoring framework-specific conventions for naming and imports

faq.question: Which languages and frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Python (pytest, unittest), TypeScript/JavaScript (Vitest, Jest), Go (testing), Rust (cargo test), Dart (flutter_test, test).

faq.question: What is the maximum number of test units per scaffold?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit. The skill extracts all public symbols. Very large files may trigger performance warnings.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with other AI Dev Kit skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It integrates with plan-phase to populate test columns and execute-lane to scaffold tests before test-engineer runs.

faq.question: Is my source code sent to external services?
faq.answer: No. This skill is a prompt definition. The AI agent reads your files locally. No data leaves your environment.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot detect my framework?
faq.answer: You must specify the framework explicitly using the TEST_FRAMEWORK variable. Without a manifest, detection fails.

faq.question: How does this compare to other test generation tools?
faq.answer: Unlike code-generation tools that write full tests, this skill creates scaffolds with TODO stubs for iterative TDD workflows.
